stem: Point F lies on a unit circle in the xy-plane and has coordinates (1, 0). Point G is the center of the circle and has coordinates (0, 0). Point H also lies on the circle and has coordinates (−1, y), where y is a constant. Which of the following could be the positive measure of angle FGH, in radians?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. Since H lies on the unit circle and has x-coordinate −1, y must be 0; therefore H = (−1, 0), diametrically opposite F. The angle FGH is π (or any value differing from π by a multiple of 2π). 17π = 8(2π) + π, so 17π is coterminal with π.

Choices A, C, and D are not coterminal with π.
